title: Why does Rei Eva 01 have blue hair
display_title: Why does Rei Eva 01 have blue hair
wikitext:
[]: Why does Rei/Eva-01 have blue hair?             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 02, 2008 10:03 am Yes, I know in reality it's because of "lol anime" (the other reasons quoted from Sadamoto are on Evapedia's Rei page) but I want to fanwank an answer. I posted this last year on ANF and was met with a load of confused responses, so I'll try to explain it better this time - The theory goes that humans, and life in general, on this planet was originally from another planet which had a much higher concentration of nitrogen in the atmosphere than the earth does. The iron in our blood helps to make it red and bonds well with the oxygen in our atmosphere, whereas on the other planet instead of iron we had copper which bonds better with nitrogen and made our blood blue - oxidised iron is red/orange (rust) and oxdised copper is blue/green. Some shellfish and crabs have blue blood. There are some books that draw a connection between the Rh- blood type (the closest to blue blood in current humans), aryan gods having blue skin like krishna/vishnu, and the world elite (previously royalty) carefully interbreeding in order to keep their blood blue - but I don't think this has much to do with the topic as it deals with "alien genes" after humans had been present on the earth for many moons. I don't know how accurate any of this is, I'm just paraphrasing from a few new agey books that talk about this. There was also a brief chat on the IRC about this, and I believe it was Bob who brought up silver poisoning permanently causing blue skin.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 02, 2008 10:25 am Where does the pigmentation found in hair feature in all this? And is Eva-01's hair actually blue, or is that just an assumption?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Apr 02, 2008 10:37 am Dude, you high?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 02, 2008 11:01 am Reichu wrote: Where does the pigmentation found in hair feature in all this?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 02, 2008 11:29 am http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hemocyanin Hmm, looks like you've been doing a little research on blue blood. Nice! I thought I'd mention this- although Krishna and Rama (and their progenitor god Vishnu) are often depicted as blue and sometimes referred to as "blue-hued", it's possible that the blue color was an artistic depiction of their dark skin, since dark skin was also associated with demonic races. "Krishna" and "Rama" literally mean "Dark One", and some depictions of Krishna show him as being jet black. I'd pick either "alien genes" or what BobBQ said about silver poisoning, rather than Gods or nobility literally having blue blood. Wikipedia gives a pretty mundane (but very logical) explanation for the phrase "blue blood". http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobility

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 02, 2008 1:05 pm Zuggy wrote: Dunno much about why hair becomes the colour it is

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 02, 2008 2:46 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Hmm, looks like you've been doing a little research on blue blood.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Apr 02, 2008 3:18 pm Where does this picture come from anyway? And how old is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 02, 2008 3:59 pm Zuggy wrote: The white on the hair looks like highlighting to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Apr 02, 2008 4:49 pm But is it official? I was just wondering if it's an old artwork or something much newer. I find it interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 02, 2008 5:16 pm It's a Yamashita piece published in a collection of concept art by him and Seito Kio. Published in 1998, though the image might have been created earlier. (Though technically the image wasn't first published in Sore o nasumono , but I can't check that right this moment...) "Official"? I dunno.

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Wed Apr 02, 2008 8:57 pm The manga extras say that Rei's eye and hair color are likely from albinism. She doesn't seem to have an aversion to sunlight, but she's seen taking an unknown medication. Not a quote,but it gets the general point of what they said.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Apr 02, 2008 9:00 pm RandomStuff wrote: The manga extras say that Rei's eye and hair color are likely from albinism.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 03, 2008 12:11 am RandomStuff wrote: The manga extras say that Rei's eye and hair color are likely from albinism.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Apr 03, 2008 6:29 pm there is no logical or scientific reason for it; she's not full-blown "albino" just very pale, which is considered a standard of beauty (see: lots of other anime) as for specifically the blue hair...the funny thing is that even the live action shot of Rei from behind in End of Eva shows the live-action Rei with a blue wig, so its kind of weird. They don't really seem to acknowledge it. I mean like in Elfen Lied, Diclonius girls have pink hair, red irises, and bone horns growing out of the tops of their heads. Everyone of course just focuses on the obvious horns; I think they kind of just assume Lucy dyes her hair or something; it's not THAT unusual to see a "pink haired girl" these days on the street, because you don't assume its her natural hair color if its a teenager....but then you see younger diclonius girls who still have pink hair; no one seems to notice their persistent red eye that much either.....nonetheless Elfen Lied does actually acknowledge in dialogue once or twice (I think maybe just in the manga) that the pink hair and red eyes are other traits of the mutation....its just that the horns are so obvious and clearly not possibly "naturally occuring" that everyone focuses on them

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 03, 2008 6:40 pm V wrote: she's not full-blown "albino"

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Mon Apr 14, 2008 9:50 pm Didn't someone on this thread say the blue is from lighting?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Apr 15, 2008 10:47 am I firmly stand by my previous statement.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Apr 15, 2008 1:25 pm Blue ink. /thread

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Tue Apr 15, 2008 7:49 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Blue ink. /thread
